verses.text: These are the commandments the LORD commanded to teach you, that ye might do them in the land. That thou mightest fear the LORD thy God, keep all His statutes — thou and thy son and thy son's son. Hear, Israel, observe to do; that it may be well with thee, that ye may increase mightily, in the land that floweth with milk and honey. Guidance: The grandchildren get a mention before the great-grandchildren are born. God's law is generational by design — not a flash-bang for one lifetime. Three generations is the minimum reading audience.

Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD. And thou shalt love the LORD thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might. Guidance: The Shema — Israel's oldest line, the one Jesus called the greatest commandment. Heart, soul, might — every drawer of the self emptied at His feet. Not most of the heart; ALL. The God who is ONE asks for love that is whole.

These words shall be in thine heart. Teach them diligently unto thy children, talk of them when thou sittest in thy house, walkest by the way, liest down, risest up. Bind them on thy hand, between thine eyes; write them on the posts of thy house and on thy gates. Guidance: Faith lives in the texture of an ordinary day — kitchen table, school walk, bedside, morning. The mezuzah on the doorpost greets you coming in and going out. Children learn what their parents TALK about, not what they say at church.

When the LORD shall have brought thee into the land — great cities thou buildedst not, houses full of good things thou filledst not, wells digged thou diggedst not, vineyards and olive trees thou plantedst not — when thou shalt have eaten and be full, then beware lest thou forget the LORD which brought thee out of Egypt. Guidance: The most dangerous moment is not the wilderness; it is the well-stocked kitchen. Full bellies forget. The houses, wells, vineyards — all gifts from God's hand into yours. Eat well; remember the Giver.

Thou shalt fear the LORD thy God, serve Him, swear by His name. Ye shall not go after other gods of the people round about you, for the LORD is a jealous God; lest the anger of the LORD be kindled and destroy thee from off the face of the earth. Guidance: Three verbs for the one God: fear, serve, swear. Three rejections: no other gods to chase, no surrounding pantheon to add Him to, no quiet drift into the neighbours' shrines. The God who carried you out of Egypt will not be one option on a shelf.

Ye shall not tempt the LORD as ye tempted Him in Massah. Diligently keep the commandments, testimonies, statutes. Do what is right and good in the sight of the LORD: that it may be well with thee, that thou mayest go in and possess the good land, cast out all thine enemies. Guidance: Massah — 'testing' — where they demanded water with a fist. Don't strong-arm God; let Him lead. 'Right and good' is the simple test: does this please Him? Obedience is not just avoiding wrong; it is choosing the right thing in daylight.

When thy son asketh, what mean these testimonies, statutes, judgments? Thou shalt say: we were Pharaoh's bondmen in Egypt; the LORD brought us out with a mighty hand, shewed signs and wonders, brought us out that He might bring us in, to give us the land He sware. The LORD commanded us to do all these statutes, for our good always, to preserve us alive. It shall be our righteousness if we observe to do all these commandments. Guidance: When the child asks WHY — tell the story. Not just the rules, the rescue. Faith is passed in narrative form: we were slaves, He brought us out, He brings us in. The catechism God writes starts with His own arm, not our merit.
